response.justification: Wei Chen is an experienced web and full-stack engineer with a strong TypeScript/Node.js background, but is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. Most critically, the candidate lists no Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — a hard requirement for this position. Beyond that disqualifying gap, the role demands deep C programming for embedded firmware, Linux kernel internals, and domain expertise in network security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, etc.), none of which appear meaningfully in this resume. The candidate's C exposure is limited to a single 6-month stint writing IDE components, and there is no evidence of embedded systems work, switch/networking experience, or security feature development.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across multiple languages and platforms, demonstrating adaptability
Some C/C++ exposure from National Instruments and a Linux-based audio thesis project, showing baseline familiarity
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, clean architecture) that would transfer to any engineering team
concerns: Zero Japanese language proficiency — a stated hard requirement that alone disqualifies the candidate
No embedded networking, LAN-switch, or Linux kernel development experience; career is almost entirely web/cloud-focused
No background in any of the required security domains: MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, or device attack/tamper detection
